reports.title: The Ceasefire That Isn't
reports.subtitle: The US and Iran are both blocking shipping and calling it peace.

reports.title: Voters Approve the Map. A Judge Says No.
reports.subtitle: Virginia's redistricting passed at the ballot box and was blocked the next morning. The midterm arms race just entered legal limbo.

reports.title: Trump Wants Rate Cuts. His Own Nominee Says He Can't Have Them.
reports.subtitle: Kevin Warsh's Fed confirmation hearing exposed a trap: he was picked to cut rates into a war-driven inflation surge that makes cutting impossible.

reports.title: SCOTUS vs. the 14th Amendment
reports.subtitle: Oral arguments in Trump v. Barbara revealed a court unlikely to rewrite a 128-year-old precedent, but the hearing exposed what the administration is actually asking for.

reports.title: Russia's Oil Unlocks Europe's Money for Ukraine
reports.subtitle: The Druzhba pipeline resumed flowing Russian crude through Ukraine, which unblocked Hungary's veto on a $105 billion EU loan to Kyiv. The pipeline is the lever that connects everything.

reports.title: Anthropic Built a Cyberweapon and Decided Not to Sell It
reports.subtitle: Mythos can chain zero-days autonomously. The NSA is using it. The Pentagon called Anthropic a supply chain risk. None of these facts fit together cleanly.

reports.title: The Tariff Refund Is a Bill, Not a Check
reports.subtitle: Businesses that passed tariff costs to consumers are now eligible for government refunds. Taxpayers pay twice and the money flows to companies that already made their customers whole.

reports.title: Social Security Is Closing Its Doors
reports.subtitle: DOGE cut 7,500 SSA employees. Offices in 12 states are now turning away walk-ins. The agency just ended phone applications too. There is nowhere left to go.

reports.title: Taiwan Sends a Minister to Its Loneliest Island
reports.subtitle: A ministerial visit to Taiping Island after seven years signals that Taiwan is quietly expanding its South China Sea posture while the world watches the Taiwan Strait.

reports.title: Google Says Agents Are the Architecture Now
reports.subtitle: Google Cloud Next 2026 was not a product launch. It was a declaration that the unit of enterprise software has changed from applications to autonomous agents.

reports.title: The Court That Stopped Explaining Itself
reports.subtitle: Leaked memos show Roberts built the shadow docket in private to kill policies he couldn't stop openly. The court's legitimacy crisis is now documented from the inside.

reports.title: The President Can't Run the Post Office. He's Trying Anyway.
reports.subtitle: Trump's March 31 executive order instructs the Postmaster General to control how states send ballots. It violates a 55-year-old law, and a coalition of AGs is now defending it.

reports.title: The EU Passed Its Toughest Russia Sanctions. Then Left Out the Main One.
reports.subtitle: Package 20 adds 46 ships to the blacklist, bans Murmansk port transactions, and promises a full maritime oil transport ban. Just not yet. The G7 has to agree first.

reports.title: The Free Trade Deal That Isn't Free Anymore
reports.subtitle: The USMCA review hits July 1 with a US Trade Representative telling Mexico its tariffs on steel, autos, and aluminum are staying. The agreement's survival now depends on what Mexico does with Chinese factories inside its borders.

reports.title: Business Is Growing. Prices Are Growing Faster.
reports.subtitle: April's flash PMI hit a 47-month high for manufacturing. It also logged the fastest input price inflation since 2022. Both facts are true. The Fed cannot act on either one.

reports.title: The White House Says China Is Copying America's AI. The Timing Is the Story.
reports.subtitle: A memo accusing China of industrial-scale AI theft lands three weeks before Trump visits Beijing. That's not a coincidence.

reports.title: The Navy Secretary Fired for Not Following Orders He Disagreed With
reports.subtitle: John Phelan thought his job was to run the Navy. Pete Hegseth thought his job was to execute Pentagon directives. One of them was right about who the boss is.

reports.title: Trump Got All 17 Pharma Companies to Say Yes. Now the Hard Part Starts.
reports.subtitle: Every major drug company has signed a most-favored-nation pledge. None of it is enforceable, and the tariff relief that made it happen just created a new exemption class.

reports.title: The EU's Toughest Russia Sanctions Required Letting Russian Oil Flow First
reports.subtitle: Package 20 is the harshest restrictions on Russia in two years. Hungary and Slovakia unlocked it only after Ukraine reopened the Druzhba pipeline. The oil is the price of the loan.

reports.title: A Jury Said Meta and Google Addicted a Child. Congress Is About to Try to Help.
reports.subtitle: The $6 million verdict in KGM v. Meta cracked Section 230 on product design. Now Britt and Fetterman are pushing Senate bills that have been stalled for a year. The courts may move faster than Congress.
